symptom: Consumers never learned an install was stale; doctor checked integrity vs the install's own manifest, never vs latest, so a 55-versions-behind install passed green forever.
consumer_impact: doctor + orient now surface an offline staleness FLOOR (age from version.json released date) nudging `maddu upgrade`.

symptom: A consumer-impacting fix could be dogfooded in-tree but shipped to consumers as the un-fixed path, with nothing coupling the change to a release that records it.
consumer_impact: release-parity gate: an impacting change carried by a version bump must record what it delivers here; pending impacting changes WARN as delivery debt.

symptom: 34 event types accumulated as 'dead' because nothing forced a disposition when a type was defined; the dead-domain surface could only ever grow.
consumer_impact: DD1: every EVENT_TYPES key carries a definition-site disposition; a gate holds the registry in 1:1 parity; DORMANT_BY_DESIGN is derived from it. insights dead count 34 -> 0.

symptom: Version skew, liveness, and the delivery delta were invisible fleet-wide; the bridge knew every workspace path but read one repo at a time.
consumer_impact: `maddu fleet`: read-only single-machine view of every registered repo's version/currency/liveness + the version delta vs fleet latest, computed offline without running them.

symptom: The cross-project audit was a manual one-off; nothing made it recurring/self-proving, so settled findings could re-surface and a guardrail could be silently removed.
consumer_impact: audit-ledger-coherent gate: a `fixed` finding must backref a registered guardrail gate, so a guardrail can't vanish while the ledger claims the class is handled.

symptom: `maddu doctor` reported a hard FAIL on a clean consumer install, telling the operator to run scripts/generate.mjs — a script that ships only in a source checkout.
consumer_impact: Gate skips on the positive source-checkout marker instead of inferring consumer-vs-source from per-target absence.

symptom: An agent built a whole feature in a fresh install with 0 sessions/lanes/slice-stops because the discipline ritual was agent-convention, not enforced.
consumer_impact: resolveSessionId precedence (flag -> env -> liveness-verified active-session cache) so one `maddu register` flows everywhere, plus `maddu hooks install` wiring SessionStart/SessionEnd.

symptom: Windows core.autocrlf rewrote all framework files to CRLF on checkout, so byte-exact integrity hashes mismatched and `upgrade` skipped every file (half-upgrade).
consumer_impact: Integrity hashing EOL-normalizes text (lossless latin1 CRLF->LF), hashes binary raw; init writes a maddu-scoped .gitattributes.

symptom: init tracked all of .maddu/ in git, so the perpetually-rewritten spine + projections churned the working tree and blocked branch switches.
consumer_impact: Policy-A .gitignore (ignore .maddu/* except durable authored artifacts) + a maddu-state-untracked gate that prints the non-destructive `git rm -r --cached` for older installs.
